extraction.one_line_summary: VALVERDE Alejandro won Stage 4 after sitting on BERNAL Egan's wheel on the final climb, refusing to pull until sprinting past him inside final 500m.
tactical_patterns: LATOUR Pierre attacked solo with 11km remaining on steep opening ramps, forcing Movistar to organize chase rather than defend.
VALVERDE refused all pulls while trailing BERNAL on final climb, parasitic tactics preserving sprint watts despite having teammates ahead.
QUINTANA invested heavily in late attacks but inadvertently dragged VALVERDE to victory, Movistar internal competition undermining team strength.
French contingent (LATOUR, BARGUIL) showed unexpected climbing form early season, signaling Tour de France peaking strategy differs from Spanish squads.

rider_signals.LATOUR Pierre: positive: Solo attack with 11km left on steep gradient demonstrates willingness to race aggressively for podium rather than defend
rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: positive: Led final climb and matched accelerations on stage not suited to him, signaling exceptional climbing endurance early career
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: negative: Multiple attacks in finale failed to drop VALVERDE, revealing inability to shed teammate despite superior position and fresher legs
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: positive: Tactical patience to refuse pulls while sitting on BERNAL wheel preserved sprint power, ruthless even against own team interests
rider_signals.YATES Simon: neutral: Finished in chase group with BARDET, neither gaining nor losing significant time on GC rivals
rider_signals.MARTIN Dan: negative: Commentary noted struggling to hold contact on final climb, suggesting declining form compared to previous Volta editions
